description: Kotlin platform types from Java can behave as nullable or non-null and need explicit boundary handling.
body: Use when Java APIs flow into Kotlin code. Do not treat a platform type as non-null without annotation or test evidence. Add explicit nullable/non-null types or guard at the boundary. Verify compile and null-path behavior. Risk is runtime NPE despite compiler success. Use when Kotlin Engineering work touches repository conventions, toolchain files, changed symbols, validation commands, and runtime output. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: use the fact to constrain the change and test plan; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include package-local checks, focused tests, build output, smoke runs, and command logs. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves broad rewrites, generated-file drift, stale assumptions, and unvalidated behavior changes, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
